Name: MS17-010: Security Update for Microsoft Windows SMB Server (4013389)
Synopsis: The remote Windows host is affected by multiple vulnerabilities in SMBv1.
Description: An remote attacker could execute arbitrary code on the target host by sending specially crafted SMBv1 packets.
Solution: Microsoft has released security updates to resolve this vulnerability. Disable SMBv1 and apply patches.

Name: Microsoft Windows Server Service Remote Code Execution (MS08-067)
Synopsis: The remote Windows host is vulnerable to a remote code execution vulnerability in the Server service.
Description: A remote attacker can execute arbitrary code with SYSTEM privileges on the affected host by sending a crafted RPC request.

Name: LNK File Automatic Remote Code Execution Vulnerability (MS10-046)
Synopsis: The remote host contains a vulnerability that allows code execution via a shortcut (.lnk) file.
Description: Windows Shell fails to properly parse shortcut files, allowing code execution when the icon is rendered.

Name: HTTP Server Header Disclosure
Synopsis: The remote web server discloses its software version in header fields.
Description: The web server headers contain details about the underlying software and OS versions, assisting target profiling.
Solution: Configure the web server to suppress the Server header (e.g. ServerTokens ProductOnly).
